V-M-076.scope: Phase-58 premium admin cockpit verification for dashboard command density, service health, users, devices, MTProto operations, MTProto analytics, tariffs, servers, analytics, confirmation-safe actions, redaction, responsive operator use, and protected-surface isolation. Phase-62 compactness/deletion audit remains planned and is not executed by this verification contract.

module-checks.check-9: Chrome screenshot audit for frontend-admin /login, /, /users, /devices, /mtproto, /servers, /plans, and /analytics at 360x740, 390x844, 430x932, 768x1024, 1440x900, plus reduced-motion / 390x844. Local pass used static route proof because no authenticated admin backend session was available; clean-deploy authenticated screenshot review remains follow-up.
module-checks.check-10: python3 XML parse for docs/modules/M-076.xml docs/plans/Phase-58.xml docs/verification/V-M-076.xml docs/verification-index.xml
module-checks.check-13: git diff --name-only HEAD -- install.sh docker-compose.yml nginx deploy deploy/mtproto-de-compose.yml .env.example backend/app backend/tests mtproto-runtime official-mtproxy frontend must be empty

verification-matrix.target-1: Admin layout keeps compact navigation, authenticated admin context, logout, route active state, and emergency surfaces reachable on phone/tablet/desktop.
verification-matrix.target-2: Dashboard first viewport exposes service health, users, devices, revenue/billing, VPN, MTProto, analytics, and incident signals without decorative bulk or horizontal scroll.
verification-matrix.target-3: Users and devices pages preserve search, filters, bounded scrolling, readable dense rows, details, block/unblock or device-control actions, and explicit confirmation for destructive/disruptive operations.
verification-matrix.target-4: MTProto and MTProto analytics preserve redacted assignment/secret/promotion-tag displays, confirmation-safe reissue/revoke/disable/block actions, alert inbox/archive, IP-history compactness, charts, top users, runtime health, and auto-refresh markers.
verification-matrix.target-5: Plans page preserves canonical M-068 tariff matrix and does not introduce accidental CRUD controls, frontend-owned checkout amounts, or catalog mutation paths.
verification-matrix.target-6: Servers and analytics pages stay operationally dense while preserving runtime-data read-only semantics, health visibility, and no backend API shape changes.
verification-matrix.target-7: Phone/tablet/desktop/reduced-motion screenshots show no text/icon/button/status overlap, no hidden emergency CTA, no long unbounded inventory, and no Matrix layer click/scroll interception.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: Admin can reach dashboard health, users, devices, MTProto, plans, servers, analytics, and incident review in one or two taps on phone.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: Users/devices/MTProto inventories are searchable, bounded, compact, and remain readable without losing row actions.
success-scenarios.scenario-3: Destructive and disruptive admin actions retain visible confirmation text, guarded buttons, and audit-safe feedback.
success-scenarios.scenario-4: MTProto secrets, full tg links, raw promotion tags when masked, access tokens, raw IP history exports, and payment/provider identifiers are not printed by smoke output, console markers, screenshots, or docs.
success-scenarios.scenario-5: Phase-58 does not touch backend admin APIs, auth/session handling, deploy/install scripts, nginx, docker-compose, MTProto/VPN runtime, or canonical tariff catalog behavior.

failure-scenarios.failure-1: Premium visual redesign hides emergency controls, destructive confirmations, MTProto reissue/revoke/disable/block actions, user/device actions, tariff state, server health, or incident review.
failure-scenarios.failure-2: Any admin route exposes MTProto secrets, full proxy links, raw promotion tags, bearer/refresh tokens, raw emails beyond synthetic fixtures, payment IDs, YooKassa/Resend keys, or backend secrets.
failure-scenarios.failure-3: Users, devices, MTProto, analytics, or plans become unbounded long-scroll surfaces without search/filter/scroll containment.
failure-scenarios.failure-4: Plans page introduces canonical tariff mutation, frontend-controlled payment amounts, or accidental CRUD controls.
failure-scenarios.failure-5: Phase-58 changes backend API shape, auth/session behavior, billing catalog values, MTProto/VPN runtime behavior, deploy/install scripts, nginx, docker-compose, or production topology.

telemetry-requirements.requirement-1: Allowed smoke output: route names, viewport names, selector markers, action names, redacted state names, screenshot paths, and pass/fail markers.
telemetry-requirements.requirement-2: Forbidden smoke output: MTProto ee secrets, full tg:// or https://t.me proxy links, raw promotion tags when masked in UI, bearer/refresh tokens, reset/verify tokens, real user emails, raw IP export data, payment IDs, YooKassa/Resend keys, database URLs, and API keys.
telemetry-requirements.requirement-3: If screenshots require authenticated fixtures, fixtures must use synthetic admin/user/device/proxy/payment data only; live clean-deploy screenshots must be operator-approved and redacted.

phase-gates.gate-1: M-076 smoke proves admin dashboard, layout, users, devices, MTProto, MTProto analytics, plans, servers, analytics, compact primitives, confirmation guards, and redaction guards.
phase-gates.gate-2: M-010, M-037, M-047, M-058, M-068, M-070, M-071, and M-072 regression smokes remain satisfied after admin cockpit edits.
phase-gates.gate-3: Frontend-admin build, Phase-58 smoke, regression smokes, screenshot/static route proof, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff check, and protected backend/deploy/runtime/frontend-user guard pass before Phase-58 can be marked done-local.
phase-gates.gate-4: Phase-62 deletion audit remains planned and must not be marked pass-local by Phase-58 evidence alone.

evidence.evidence-1: 2026-06-04 pass-local: Phase-58 smoke printed all required PremiumAdminCockpit markers including routes, command center, actions, bounded inventories, MTProto redaction, confirmation guards, tariff guard, readonly analytics, reduced motion, static screenshot proof, and protected-surface guard.
evidence.evidence-2: Regression evidence passed: Phase-42 MTProto admin analytics smoke, Phase-43 MTProto analytics UX/IP/alerts smoke, Phase-50 tariff catalog smoke, Phase-52 Matrix visual smoke, Phase-54 admin Matrix smoke, and Phase-55 premium art-direction smoke.
evidence.evidence-3: Protected-surface evidence passed: no diff in install.sh, docker-compose.yml, nginx, deploy, deploy/mtproto-de-compose.yml, .env.example, backend/app, backend/tests, mtproto-runtime, official-mtproxy, or frontend.

V-M-076.autonomous-execution-safety: Safe for local autonomous execution if changes stay inside frontend-admin cockpit routes/components/CSS, Phase-58 smoke verification, and MyGRACE docs. Stop execution if backend admin API changes, auth/session changes, billing catalog mutation, VPN/MTProto runtime changes, deploy/install/nginx/docker-compose changes, user frontend changes, or secret-handling changes appear necessary.
